title: Listening - Part 3: Community Classes
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about their reasons for attending a community class. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the reason each speaker gives. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about their reasons for attending a community class. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the reason each speaker gives. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about why they decided to attend a community class.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: My cousin and I are travelling around South America next summer. At first I assumed we could manage with an app, but she pointed out that we plan to visit several small towns. [19]I enrolled on the Spanish course because I want to ask for information and chat to people during the trip, without depending on my phone all the time.[/19] I'm still slow, but I can already manage simple conversations.||Speaker 2 Man: I moved into this area in January and work from home, which meant I didn't naturally come across many people. I enjoy plants, so the gardening course seemed a reasonable choice, even though my balcony is tiny. [20]To be honest, the main attraction was meeting others who live nearby and perhaps feeling more part of the neighbourhood.[/20] We've now started exchanging plants and sometimes meet for coffee after class.||Speaker 3 Woman: I use my bike to get everywhere, and it always seemed to need some little repair: brakes adjusting, a new inner tube or the chain sorting out. None of the jobs cost a fortune individually, but together they added up. [21]The bicycle-maintenance class appealed to me because I could stop paying a repair shop each time something simple went wrong.[/21] I've already fixed a puncture by myself, which felt surprisingly satisfying.||Speaker 4 Man: I work in a busy hospital reception area. By the end of a shift, I often find I'm still going over difficult conversations in my head. A colleague suggested photography, although I'd never owned anything more complicated than a phone camera. [22]When I'm concentrating on light and colour in the evening class, I completely forget the pressures of the working day.[/22] That's the real benefit for me, rather than producing perfect pictures.||Speaker 5 Woman: I've been working at a hotel for three years, mostly at the front desk. Recently an assistant manager's position became available, but it involved keeping digital records and producing reports, which I'd never done. [23]I chose the computing course because gaining those skills should put me in a stronger position when I apply for promotion next time.[/23] It's demanding, but my manager has already noticed that I'm quicker with spreadsheets.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: My cousin and I are travelling around South America next summer. At first I assumed we could manage with an app, but she pointed out that we plan to visit several small towns. I enrolled on the Spanish course because I want to ask for information and chat to people during the trip, without depending on my phone all the time. I'm still slow, but I can already manage simple conversations.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: I moved into this area in January and work from home, which meant I didn't naturally come across many people. I enjoy plants, so the gardening course seemed a reasonable choice, even though my balcony is tiny. To be honest, the main attraction was meeting others who live nearby and perhaps feeling more part of the neighbourhood. We've now started exchanging plants and sometimes meet for coffee after class.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: I use my bike to get everywhere, and it always seemed to need some little repair: brakes adjusting, a new inner tube or the chain sorting out. None of the jobs cost a fortune individually, but together they added up. The bicycle-maintenance class appealed to me because I could stop paying a repair shop each time something simple went wrong. I've already fixed a puncture by myself, which felt surprisingly satisfying.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I work in a busy hospital reception area. By the end of a shift, I often find I'm still going over difficult conversations in my head. A colleague suggested photography, although I'd never owned anything more complicated than a phone camera. When I'm concentrating on light and colour in the evening class, I completely forget the pressures of the working day. That's the real benefit for me, rather than producing perfect pictures.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: I've been working at a hotel for three years, mostly at the front desk. Recently an assistant manager's position became available, but it involved keeping digital records and producing reports, which I'd never done. I chose the computing course because gaining those skills should put me in a stronger position when I apply for promotion next time. It's demanding, but my manager has already noticed that I'm quicker with spreadsheets.

options.text: to improve their prospects at work
to get to know people locally
to reduce the cost of everyday life
to prepare for a trip abroad
to take part in a public competition
to deal with a local environmental problem

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker is learning Spanish so she can communicate independently while travelling in South America (evidence [19]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The speaker joined mainly in order to meet neighbours and feel part of the local area (evidence [20]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The speaker wanted to save money by learning to do minor bicycle repairs herself (evidence [21]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The class helps the speaker stop thinking about a stressful working day (evidence [22]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker is acquiring computing skills in order to be considered for promotion (evidence [23]).
